options.name: death_link
options.description: When you die, everyone who enabled death link dies. Of course, the reverse is true too.

options.name: progression_balancing
options.description:
A system that can move progression earlier, to try and prevent the player from getting stuck and bored early.

A lower setting means more getting stuck. A higher setting means less getting stuck.

options.name: accessibility
options.description:
Set rules for reachability of your items/locations.

**Full:** ensure everything can be reached and acquired.

**Minimal:** ensure what is needed to reach your goal can be acquired.

options.name: start_with_jewelry_box
options.description: Start with Jewelry Box unlocked

options.name: downloadable_items
options.description: With the tablet you will be able to download items at terminals

options.name: eye_spy
options.description: Requires Oculus Ring in inventory to be able to break hidden walls.

options.name: start_with_meyef
options.description: Start with Meyef, ideal for when you want to play multiplayer.

options.name: quick_seed
options.description: Start with Talaria Attachment, Nyoom!

options.name: specific_keycards
options.description: Keycards can only open corresponding doors

options.name: inverted
options.description: Start in the past

options.name: gyre_archives
options.description: Gyre locations are in logic. New warps are gated by Merchant Crow and Kobo

options.name: cantoran
options.description: Cantoran's fight and check are available upon revisiting his room

options.name: lore_checks
options.description: Memories and journal entries contain items.

options.name: boss_rando
options.description: Wheter all boss locations are shuffled, and if their damage/hp should be scaled.

options.name: boss_rando_type
options.description:
Sets what type of boss shuffling occurs.
Shuffle: Bosses will be shuffled amongst each other
Chaos: Bosses will be randomized with the chance of duplicate bosses
Singularity: All bosses will be replaced with a single boss
Manual: Bosses will be placed according to the Boss Rando Overrides setting

options.name: boss_rando_overrides
options.description:
Manual mapping of bosses to the boss they will be replaced with.
Bosses that you don't specify will be the vanilla boss.

options.name: enemy_rando
options.description: Wheter enemies will be randomized, and if their damage/hp should be scaled.

options.name: damage_rando
options.description: Randomly nerfs and buffs some orbs and their associated spells as well as some associated rings.

options.name: damage_rando_overrides
options.description:
Manual +/-/normal odds for an orb. Put 0 if you don't want a certain nerf or buff to be a possibility. Orbs that
you don't specify will roll with 1/1/1 as odds

options.name: hp_cap
options.description: Sets the number that Lunais's HP maxes out at.

options.name: aura_cap
options.description: Sets the maximum Aura Lunais is allowed to have. Level 1 is 80. Djinn Inferno costs 45.

options.name: level_cap
options.description: Sets the max level Lunais can achieve.

options.name: extra_earrings_xp
options.description: Adds additional XP granted by Galaxy Earrings.

options.name: boss_healing
options.description: Enables/disables healing after boss fights. NOTE: Currently only applicable when Boss Rando is enabled.

options.name: shop_fill
options.description:
Sets the items for sale in Merchant Crow's shops.
Default: No sunglasses or trendy jacket, but sand vials for sale.
Randomized: Up to 4 random items in each shop.
Vanilla: Keep shops the same as the base game.
Empty: Sell no items at the shop.

options.name: shop_warp_shards
options.description: Shops always sell warp shards (when keys possessed), ignoring inventory setting.

options.name: shop_multiplier
options.description: Multiplier for the cost of items in the shop. Set to 0 for free shops.

options.name: loot_pool
options.description:
Sets the items that drop from enemies (does not apply to boss reward checks)
Vanilla: Drops are the same as the base game
Randomized: Each slot of every enemy's drop table is given a random use item or piece of equipment.
Empty: Enemies drop nothing.

options.name: drop_rate_category
options.description:
Sets the drop rate when 'Loot Pool' is set to 'Random'
Tiered: Based on item rarity/value
Vanilla: Based on bestiary slot the item is placed into
Random: Assigned a random tier/drop rate
Fixed: Set by the 'Fixed Drop Rate' setting

options.name: fixed_drop_rate
options.description: Base drop rate percentage when 'Drop Rate Category' is set to 'Fixed'

options.name: loot_tier_distro
options.description:
Sets how often items of each rarity tier are placed when 'Loot Pool' is set to 'Random'
Default Weight: Rarer items will be assigned to enemy drop slots less frequently than common items
Full Random: Any item has an equal chance of being placed in an enemy's drop slot
Inverted Weight: Rarest items show up the most frequently, while common items are the rarest

options.name: show_bestiary
options.description: All entries in the bestiary are visible, without needing to kill one of a given enemy first

options.name: show_drops
options.description: All item drops in the bestiary are visible, without needing an enemy to drop one of a given item first

options.name: enter_sandman
options.description: The Ancient Pyramid is unlocked by the Twin Pyramid Keys, but the final boss door opens if you have all 5 Timespinner pieces

options.name: dad_percent
options.description: The win condition is beating the boss of Emperor's Tower

options.name: rising_tides
options.description: Random areas are flooded or drained, can be further specified with RisingTidesOverrides

options.name: rising_tides_overrides
options.description:
Odds for specific areas to be flooded or drained, only has effect when RisingTides is on.
Areas that are not specified will roll with the default 33% chance of getting flooded or drained

options.name: unchained_keys
options.description:
Start with Twin Pyramid Key, which does not give free warp;
warp items for Past, Present, (and ??? with Enter Sandman) can be found.

options.name: back_to_the_future
options.description: When inverted, allows using the refugee camp warp when both the Timespinner Wheel and Spindle is acquired.

options.name: prism_break
options.description:
Adds 3 Laser Access items to the item pool to remove the lasers blocking the military hangar area
instead of needing to beat the Golden Idol, Aelana, and The Maw.

options.name: lock_key_amadeus
options.description: Lasers in Amadeus' Laboratory are disabled via items, rather than by de-powering the lab. Experiments will spawn in the lab.

options.name: risky_warps
options.description: Expanded free-warp eligible locations, including Azure Queen, Xarion, Amadeus' Laboratory, and Emperor's Tower.

options.name: pyramid_start
options.description: Start in ???. Takes priority over Inverted. Additional chests in Dark Forest and Pyramid. Sandman door behaves as it does in Enter Sandman.

options.name: gate_keep
options.description: The castle drawbridge starts raised, and can be lowered via item.

options.name: royal_roadblock
options.description: The Royal Towers entrance door requires a royal orb (Plasma Orb, Plasma Geyser, or Royal Ring) to enter.

options.name: pure_torcher
options.description: All lanterns contain checks. (Except tutorial)

options.name: find_the_flame
options.description: Lanterns in 'Pure Torcher' will not break without new item 'Cube of Bodie'.

options.name: trap_chance
options.description:
Chance of traps in the item pool.
Traps will only replace filler items such as potions, vials and antidotes

options.name: traps
options.description: List of traps that may be in the item pool to find

options.description: Start with Jewelry Box unlocked

options.name: DownloadableItems
options.description: With the tablet you will be able to download items at terminals

options.description: Requires Oculus Ring in inventory to be able to break hidden walls.

options.name: local_items
options.description: Forces these items to be in their native world.

options.name: non_local_items
options.description: Forces these items to be outside their native world.

options.name: start_inventory
options.description: Start with the specified amount of these items. Example: "Bomb: 1"

options.name: start_hints
options.description: Start with these item's locations prefilled into the ``!hint`` command.

options.name: start_location_hints
options.description: Start with these locations and their item prefilled into the ``!hint`` command.
